meals.name: Mexican Street Corn Pasta Salad
ings: 3 cups cooked rotini or penne pasta
steps: In a large bowl, combine the cooked and cooled pasta with corn.
In a separate bowl, whisk together mayonnaise, sour cream, lime juice, chili powder, paprika, garlic powder, salt, and pepper.
Pour the dressing over the pasta mixture and toss until fully coated.
Fold in chopped cilantro and cotija cheese.Salad dressing guide
Chill in the refrigerator for 30 minutes before serving.
meals.note: Use grilled corn for the most flavor. Add diced jalapeños or hot sauce for extra heat. Great make-ahead dish for BBQs and potlucks.
meals.label: Meal A

meals.name: Spicy Garlic Chicken Bowl with Hummus
ings: 1 lb boneless skinless chicken thighs, cubed
1 tsp paprika, ½ tsp cayenne, 1 tsp cumin
Cooked rice or quinoa for serving
steps: Toss chicken with oil, garlic, paprika, cayenne, cumin, salt, and pepper.
Heat skillet over medium-high; cook chicken until browned and cooked through, 8–10 min.
Combine cucumber, red onion, parsley, and lemon juice for the salad.
Divide rice or quinoa among bowls. Top with chicken, a scoop of hummus, and the cucumber salad. Serve immediately.
meals.label: Meal B

meals.name: Marry Me Chicken Gnocchi
ings: 1¼ lb boneless skinless chicken thighs or breasts, cut into bite-sized pieces
2 Tbsp olive oil (or oil from the sun-dried tomato jar)
1 small shallot or ½ onion, diced
⅔ cup sun-dried tomatoes (oil-packed), drained and chopped
1 cup half-and-half (or ¾ cup heavy cream)
Red pepper flakes (optional, for adults only)
steps: Season chicken with salt and pepper. Heat 1 Tbsp oil in a large high-sided skillet over medium-high. Sear chicken in a single layer 3–4 min per side until golden (doesn't need to be cooked through). Remove and set aside.
Add remaining oil and shallot to the skillet; sauté 2–3 min. Add garlic and sun-dried tomatoes; cook 1 min until fragrant.
Add uncooked gnocchi, breaking apart any stuck pieces. Pour in chicken broth and half-and-half; season with salt and pepper. Cover and cook over medium 5 min until gnocchi are tender and fluffy.
Return chicken to the pan. Add spinach; cook uncovered 3–5 min until chicken is cooked through and spinach is wilted.
Stir in Parmesan until melted and the sauce is creamy. Top with torn basil. Serve with red pepper flakes on the side for adults.
meals.note: 💡 The base recipe is mild — keep the red pepper flakes off to the side so the kids can eat it. Gnocchi cooks right in the sauce, no boiling needed.
meals.label: Meal C
